questions.question: What is MongoDB?
options: B) A document-oriented NoSQL database storing BSON-like documents
questions.correctAnswer: B) A document-oriented NoSQL database storing BSON-like documents

questions.question: What is BSON in MongoDB?
options: B) Binary JSON format used internally by MongoDB with support for additional data types
questions.correctAnswer: B) Binary JSON format used internally by MongoDB with support for additional data types

questions.question: What is the difference between a collection and a document?
options: A) A collection is a single record
B) A collection stores multiple documents; a document is one BSON record
D) Documents can only exist in arrays
questions.correctAnswer: B) A collection stores multiple documents; a document is one BSON record

questions.question: What is the main tradeoff when embedding versus referencing related data?
options: A) Embedding always uses less space
B) Embedding favors read locality; referencing favors normalization and independent updates
questions.correctAnswer: B) Embedding favors read locality; referencing favors normalization and independent updates

questions.question: What does the _id field represent?
options: B) Unique identifier automatically added to each MongoDB document unless provided
questions.correctAnswer: B) Unique identifier automatically added to each MongoDB document unless provided

questions.question: What is the purpose of an index in MongoDB?
options: B) To speed up data retrieval at the cost of extra storage and slower writes
questions.correctAnswer: B) To speed up data retrieval at the cost of extra storage and slower writes

questions.question: Why can too many indexes hurt performance?
options: A) They make reads slower only
B) Every write may need to update multiple indexes, increasing storage and write cost
questions.correctAnswer: B) Every write may need to update multiple indexes, increasing storage and write cost

questions.question: What does a compound index depend on heavily?
options: B) Column or field order in the index matching query patterns
questions.correctAnswer: B) Column or field order in the index matching query patterns

questions.question: What is the aggregation pipeline used for?
options: B) Transforming and analyzing documents in staged operations like match, group, project, and lookup
questions.correctAnswer: B) Transforming and analyzing documents in staged operations like match, group, project, and lookup

questions.question: What does $match do in an aggregation pipeline?
options: B) Filters documents, similar to query conditions
questions.correctAnswer: B) Filters documents, similar to query conditions

questions.question: What does $group do?
options: B) Groups documents by a key and computes aggregate values
questions.correctAnswer: B) Groups documents by a key and computes aggregate values

questions.question: What is $lookup in MongoDB?
options: B) An aggregation stage used to join data from another collection
questions.correctAnswer: B) An aggregation stage used to join data from another collection

questions.question: Why should schema validation still matter in a schema-flexible database?
options: B) It helps preserve data quality and reduce inconsistent document structures
questions.correctAnswer: B) It helps preserve data quality and reduce inconsistent document structures

questions.question: What is a replica set in MongoDB?
options: A) A set of duplicate indexes
B) A group of mongod processes maintaining the same data for redundancy and failover
questions.correctAnswer: B) A group of mongod processes maintaining the same data for redundancy and failover

questions.question: What is a transaction in MongoDB used for?
options: B) Grouping multiple operations into an atomic unit, especially across documents or collections
questions.correctAnswer: B) Grouping multiple operations into an atomic unit, especially across documents or collections

questions.question: Why are transactions generally used more cautiously in MongoDB than in some relational designs?
options: B) Good document modeling often avoids needing many multi-document transactions
D) Transactions only work on empty collections
questions.correctAnswer: B) Good document modeling often avoids needing many multi-document transactions

questions.question: What is sharding?
options: B) Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple machines based on a shard key
questions.correctAnswer: B) Horizontal partitioning of data across multiple machines based on a shard key

questions.question: What makes a good shard key important?
options: B) It affects distribution, hotspot risk, and scalability characteristics
C) It removes need for indexes
questions.correctAnswer: B) It affects distribution, hotspot risk, and scalability characteristics

questions.question: What does explain() help with in MongoDB?
options: B) Inspecting query execution plans to understand index usage and performance
questions.correctAnswer: B) Inspecting query execution plans to understand index usage and performance

questions.question: What is the role of Mongoose in a Node.js app?
options: B) An ODM that helps define schemas, models, validation, middleware, and query helpers for MongoDB
questions.correctAnswer: B) An ODM that helps define schemas, models, validation, middleware, and query helpers for MongoDB

questions.question: What is the default port of MongoDB?
Which MongoDB command inserts data?
questions.correctAnswer: B) insertOne()
